mnemonics: PRODUCT is the final OUTPUT, PROCESS is the PATH we take
keyPoints: Product Quality Management focuses on the attributes of the final deliverable (e.g., functionality, reliability, usability).
Process Quality Management focuses on the activities used to create the product (e.g., methodologies, standards, reviews).
A high-quality process is the most reliable way to consistently produce a high-quality product.
commonMistakes: Focusing solely on the final product and ignoring the process that created it, leading to inconsistent results.
Assuming a high-quality process automatically guarantees a high-quality product, without adequate testing and validation.
